item.text: {"summary":"I’ll inspect the requested `main...HEAD` diff only and keep this to static review, without running builds or tests.","overall_severity":"none","findings":[]}

{"summary":"Git is emitting macOS cache warnings under the read-only sandbox, but the diff command is still running. I’m polling its output and will only use the diff content for the review.","overall_severity":"none","findings":[]}

diff --git a/calc.go b/calc.go
index 25506a9..ca74c2e 100644
--- a/calc.go
+++ b/calc.go
@@ -3,9 +3,19 @@ package main
 import "fmt"
 
 func divide(a, b int) int {
+	// BUG: no zero check, will panic
 	return a / b
 }
 
+func process(values []int) int {
+	total := 0
+	for i := 0; i <= len(values); i++ { // BUG: off-by-one, index out of range
+		total += values[i]
+	}
+	return total
+}
+
 func main() {
-	fmt.Println(divide(10, 2))
+	fmt.Println(divide(10, 0))
+	fmt.Println(process([]int{1, 2, 3}))
 }

item.text: {"summary":"The diff introduces two unconditional runtime panics in the executable path: `main` divides by zero, and the new slice processing loop indexes one past the end of the slice. No builds or tests were run per instruction.","overall_severity":"high","findings":[{"path":"calc.go","line":19,"side":"NEW","severity":"high","title":"Program now always panics by dividing by zero","body":"`main` changed the call from `divide(10, 2)` to `divide(10, 0)`. Since `divide` directly evaluates `a / b`, this causes an immediate integer divide-by-zero panic at runtime and prevents the program from reaching the subsequent processing call."},{"path":"calc.go","line":12,"side":"NEW","severity":"high","title":"Loop condition indexes one past the slice bounds","body":"The new `process` loop uses `i <= len(values)`, so the final iteration executes with `i == len(values)` and `values[i]` is out of range. This will panic for any input slice, including the `[]int{1, 2, 3}` passed from `main`; the condition should stop before `len(values)`."}]}
